political.s: General Mills demonstrates right-leaning political leanings with PAC donations showing 87.6% Republican bias (35,500 of 40,500 in verified data). The company maintains a transparent PAC structure run by employees and lobbied $1.27M in 2024, with some strategic shifts including suspension of donations to election deniers in 2021.

charity.s: General Mills demonstrates substantial charitable commitment with approximately $98M+ in annual giving focused on food security, regenerative agriculture, and community development. The company donated 52.2M pounds of surplus food in 2024 and supported 7.9 billion meals through philanthropic partners, earning recognition as one of America's Most Community-Minded Companies for ten consecutive years.
charity.focus: Food security, hunger relief, regenerative agriculture, education, community development

environment.s: General Mills demonstrates strong environmental performance with 14% reduction in total value chain emissions and 55% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions through 2025. The company achieved zero waste to landfill at owned facilities, engaged 800,000+ acres in regenerative agriculture programs, and maintains 95% recyclable/reusable packaging by weight.

labor.s: General Mills maintains mixed labor practices with several NLRB cases on record but no major documented violations. The company reports positive workplace satisfaction and belonging scores through employee programs, though CEO-to-worker pay ratio of approximately 416:1 significantly exceeds S&P 500 median of 324:1.

dei.s: General Mills maintains strong pro-DEI stance with perfect 100 scores on HRC Corporate Equality Index for 16+ consecutive years and Glassdoor DEI rating of 4.3/5 (27.6% above manufacturing sector average). The company operates six employee networks covering Black, Asian, Latino, LGBTQ+, Women, Veterans, and Disability communities with documented leadership commitment.

animals.s: Not applicable - General Mills is a packaged food company without animal testing operations.

guns.s: Not applicable - General Mills manufactures and markets branded consumer foods and does not engage in firearms sales or manufacturing.

privacy.s: General Mills maintains mixed data privacy practices. Company collects consumer data through its digital platforms and products but publicly provides limited detailed privacy disclosures. Privacy assessments from independent sources are not prominently available.

execPay.s: General Mills demonstrates poor executive pay ratio with CEO compensation of approximately $12.2M compared to median worker pay, resulting in approximately 416:1 ratio significantly exceeding S&P 500 median of 324:1. CEO salary comprises only 10.8% with 89.2% derived from bonuses and stock compensation.
